descriptions.Jackfruit: Large, starchy tropical fruit with a meaty texture; used in curries, desserts, and as a meat substitute. Strongly favoured by South and Southeast Asian shoppers.
descriptions.Soursop (Guan\u00e1bana): Creamy, tangy tropical fruit used in juices, smoothies, and desserts. Popular across Caribbean, Latin American, and Southeast Asian communities.
descriptions.Dragonfruit (Pitaya): Visually striking fruit with vibrant pink skin and mild, sweet flesh. Trending with younger shoppers; popular across East and Southeast Asian cuisines.
descriptions.Starfruit (Carambola): Sweet-tart, crisp tropical fruit eaten fresh or as a garnish. Common in Southeast Asian and South Asian cooking; low calorie and visually appealing.
descriptions.Tomatillos: Tangy, husk-covered green fruit essential to Mexican salsas, sauces, and stews. A cornerstone ingredient in Hispanic/Latino kitchens.
descriptions.Prickly Pear: Sweet, watermelon-like cactus fruit used in juices, syrups, and traditional Mexican cuisine. Increasingly on-trend for beverages and cocktails.
descriptions.Lychee: Fragrant, sweet fruit with floral notes and juicy flesh. Eaten fresh and used in desserts and drinks; widely consumed across East and Southeast Asian households.
descriptions.Sitafal (Sugar Apple): Creamy, custard-like tropical fruit with a sweet, aromatic flavour. A staple in South Asian households, particularly Indian and Sri Lankan communities.
